mission.title: woodpecker-ci: `2.4.1` backend docker run error `cannot create /root/.netrc: Permission denied`
mission.description: `2.4.1` backend docker run error `cannot create /root/.netrc: Permission denied`. This issue affects 6+ users.

steps.title: Identify woodpecker-ci troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your woodpecker-ci deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n woodpecker-ci -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci -n woodpecker-ci --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review woodpecker-ci configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant woodpecker-ci configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n woodpecker-ci -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci
kubectl get configmap -n woodpecker-ci -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=woodpecker-ci
```
### Component

server, agent

### Describe the bug

```yml
labels: # https://woodpecker-ci.org/docs/usage/workflow-syntax#labels
  backend: docker
  platform: linux/amd64

steps:
  python-poetry-gcc:
    image: fnndsc/python-poetry:1.7.1
    pull:

steps.title: Apply the fix for `2.4.1` backend docker run error `cannot createâ€¦
steps.description:
Setting `workingDir` to `workspaceBase`, i.e. `/woodpecker`, and creating `<workspaceBase>/src/<repo slug>` via the container user solves this. The subsequently created dir is then owned by the container user and all operations work.
To achieve this, `workspaceBase` has been added to the `Step`
```yaml
labels: # https://woodpecker-ci.org/docs/usage/workflow-syntax#labels
  backend: docker
  platform: linux/amd64

steps:
  python-poetry-gcc:
    image: fnndsc/python-poetry:1.7.1
    pull: false
    commands:
      - whoami
      - id
```

steps.title: Confirm `2.4.1` backend docker run error `cannot createâ€¦ is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci -n woodpecker-ci --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n woodpecker-ci --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Setting `workingDir` to `workspaceBase`, i.e. `/woodpecker`, and creating `<workspaceBase>/src/<repo slug>` via the container user solves this. The subsequently created dir is then owned by the container user and all operations work.
To achieve this, `workspaceBase` has been added to the `Step` struct and set as `workingDir` during pod creation.
codeSnippets:
labels: # https://woodpecker-ci.org/docs/usage/workflow-syntax#labels
  backend: docker
  platform: linux/amd64

steps:
  python-poetry-gcc:
    image: fnndsc/python-poetry:1.7.1
    pull: false
    commands:
      - whoami
      - id

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with woodpecker-ci installed or the issue environment reproducible.
